Etiology: HBV (chronic hepatitis B-induced HCC; adjacent normal tissue paired)
Tissue type: HCC tumor and paired adjacent/non-tumor liver
Use in analysis: Discovery cohort; DEG identification across platforms
Inclusion/exclusion rationale: Included; Large HBV-specific HCC cohort; paired tumor/adjacent design; widely used as benchmark on Affymetrix GPL570; enables HBV-focused subgroup analyses
Included; Balanced paired design (20 tumor vs 20 adjacent non-tumor); same platform as GSE121248; adds independent replication for Affymetrix meta-analysis

Tissue type: Chronic HBV hepatitis liver and healthy liver; no HCC tumors
Use in analysis: Pre-neoplastic HBV liver transcriptome reference; hepatitis-to-HCC progression context
Inclusion/exclusion rationale: Included for HBV injury-axis derivation only; excluded from tumor/non-tumor HCC validation.

Etiology: HBV-associated acute liver failure (ALF); 4 patients, massive or submassive hepatic necrosis
Tissue type: HBV-associated acute liver failure explanted liver and normal donor liver; no HCC tumors
Use in analysis: Reference for HBV-driven extreme liver injury; tumorigenesis gene signature in non-HCC context
Inclusion/exclusion rationale: Included only as contextual HBV liver-injury reference; excluded from HCC tumor/non-tumor validation.

Etiology: HBV (predominant; BCLC stages 0–C; Fudan University, Shanghai cohort)
Use in analysis: Training/validation of prognostic signatures; benchmark for clinical annotation analyses
Inclusion/exclusion rationale: Included; Largest HCC GEO microarray dataset; comprehensive clinical metadata (AFP, BCLC stage, survival); gold-standard benchmark; HBV-dominant cohort with full staging information

Etiology: Mixed etiology (HBV, HCV, alcohol; US multicenter cohort)
Tissue type: HCC tumor and non-tumor/adjacent/healthy/cirrhotic liver where labels were recoverable
Use in analysis: Multi-etiology DEG discovery; training set for machine-learning biomarker models
Inclusion/exclusion rationale: Included; One of the largest HCC GEO expression datasets; multi-etiology cohort enabling pan-etiology signature derivation; unique Rosetta platform adds cross-platform diversity for robustness testing

Etiology: HBV-predominant (Samsung Medical Center, Seoul, Korea; 2000–2006; Child-Pugh A)
Use in analysis: DEG discovery; hub gene identification; cross-platform validation with Affymetrix datasets
Inclusion/exclusion rationale: Included; Largest Illumina-based HCC microarray dataset; Korean HBV-endemic cohort; well-characterized clinical data; widely used as benchmark for Illumina-platform meta-analyses

Use in analysis: DEG discovery; external validation of prognostic gene signatures
Inclusion/exclusion rationale: Included; Paired design (52 matched tumor/adjacent non-tumor + 63 additional tumors); same Illumina platform as GSE36376 and GSE57957; Singapore multi-etiology cohort provides geographic diversity
Included; Perfectly balanced paired design (1:1 tumor/adjacent); same Illumina platform as GSE36376/GSE76427; enables direct platform-controlled meta-analysis without batch correction artifacts

Use in analysis: DEG identification; validation of Affymetrix-derived gene signatures
Inclusion/exclusion rationale: Included; Adds Affymetrix GPL570 cohort for cross-study overlap analysis; contributes to multi-cohort DEG consensus filtering alongside GSE121248; independently derived from different institution

Etiology: Not specified (Japanese cohort, Shimada et al. 2019)
Tissue type: HCC tumor and limited adjacent/non-tumor liver
Use in analysis: External validation of expression signatures; large tumor transcriptome reference set
Inclusion/exclusion rationale: Included; Largest tumor sample count (n=183) among Affymetrix GPL570 datasets in this set; Japanese cohort adds geographic and genetic diversity; strong statistical power for validation despite low non-tumor n (15)

Tissue type: Primary HCC tumor RNA-seq with clinical follow-up; solid normal samples parsed when available
n tumor: 369 tumor cases with survival data in Cox models
n non-tumor: solid normal samples parsed when available; not used for survival modeling
Use in analysis: RNA-seq-based module scoring and overall-survival Cox modeling
Inclusion/exclusion rationale: Included for independent RNA-seq prognostic-coherence assessment and clinical modeling; not used to derive GEO microarray modules.
